attacking_style: Powerful diesel rouleur who attacks solo from breakaways on moderate climbs or mid-race (typically 60km+ out), relying on sustained pace rather than explosive acceleration. Frequently goes solo at 1.5-5km from breakaway groups to exploit time trial strength.
team_tactics: Quick-Step/Deceuninck uses him as sacrificial domestique for early race control, willing to burn him at record pace to disrupt rival leadout trains or protect teammates like Bennett/Alaphilippe. Rarely receives direct team support when attacking solo.
terrain_preference: Moderate climbs and rolling terrain where sustained power trumps punch. Excels in breakaway scenarios on moderate gradients; struggles on punchy climbs requiring repeated accelerations. Corniche-style circuits and time trial-like efforts suit best.
vulnerability: Cannot sustain efforts on punchy climbs after initial surge—cracks completely when caught after solo attacks. Reputation now triggers immediate peloton response even with minimal gaps, preventing breakaway tolerance. Prone to going too hard too early (unsustainable 49kph+ averages leading to fade).
key_patterns: Attacks solo from breakaways at distance (5+ instances 60km-120km out), signature early move pattern.
Used tactically by Quick-Step to disrupt rival sprint trains through suicidal early pace (4 instances).
Immediately marked by peloton despite small gaps due to reputation, preventing breakaway tolerance (3 instances).
Collects intermediate bonus seconds showing GC awareness even in stage-hunting mode (2 instances).
Fades after unsustainable early efforts or cracks when caught after solo surges (3 instances).
Goes solo from small breakaways on final climb/circuit rather than contesting group sprint (4 instances).
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 showing unsustainable pacing leading to fade and isolated attacks gaining minimal time, suggesting declining capacity compared to 2019-2020 record-breaking Tour efforts
